summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding PostgreSQL resources to an

members.name: AddDatabase
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<PostgresDatabaseResource> PostgresBuilderExtensions.AddDatabase(this IResourceBuilder<PostgresServerResource> builder, string name, string? databaseName = null)

summary.text: Adds a PostgreSQL database to the application model.

children.text: This resource includes built-in health checks. When this resource is referenced as a dependency using the
extension method then the dependent resource will wait until the Postgres database is available.
will result in the database being created on the Postgres server when the server becomes ready. The database creation happens automatically as part of the resource lifecycle.

databaseName.text: The name of the database. If not provided, this defaults to the same value as

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

members.name: AddPostgres
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<PostgresServerResource> PostgresBuilderExtensions.AddPostgres(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? userName = null, IResourceBuilder<ParameterResource>? password = null, int? port = null)

summary.text: Adds a PostgreSQL resource to the application model. A container is used for local development.

children.text: This resource includes built-in health checks. When this resource is referenced as a dependency using the
extension method then the dependent resource will wait until the Postgres resource is able to service requests.

remarks.text: This version of the package defaults to the

name.text: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.

password.text: The parameter used to provide the administrator password for the PostgreSQL resource. If
a random password will be generated.

port.text: The host port used when launching the container. If null a random port will be assigned.

userName.text: The parameter used to provide the user name for the PostgreSQL resource. If
a default value will be used.

summary.text: Defines the SQL script used to create the database.

remarks.text: The script can only contain SQL statements applying to the default database like CREATE DATABASE. Custom statements like table creation and data insertion are not supported since they require a distinct connection to the newly created database.

script.text: The SQL script used to create the database.

members.name: WithDataBindMount
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<PostgresServerResource> PostgresBuilderExtensions.WithDataBindMount(this IResourceBuilder<PostgresServerResource> builder, string source, bool isReadOnly = false)

summary.text: Adds a bind mount for the data folder to a PostgreSQL container resource.

children.text: The data directory location varies by PostgreSQL version:
This method automatically selects the correct path based on the configured container image tag.

isReadOnly.text: A flag that indicates if this is a read-only mount.

source.text: The source directory on the host to mount into the container.

members.name: WithDataVolume
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<PostgresServerResource> PostgresBuilderExtensions.WithDataVolume(this IResourceBuilder<PostgresServerResource> builder, string? name = null, bool isReadOnly = false)

summary.text: Adds a named volume for the data folder to a PostgreSQL container resource.

children.text: The data directory location varies by PostgreSQL version:
This method automatically selects the correct path based on the configured container image tag.

isReadOnly.text: A flag that indicates if this is a read-only volume.

name.text: The name of the volume. Defaults to an auto-generated name based on the application and resource names.

summary.text: Configures the host port that the PGAdmin resource is exposed on instead of using randomly assigned port.

port.text: The port to bind on the host. If
is used random port will be assigned.
